data.title: Matex International Concludes Lawsuits, Trading Suspension Continues
data.description:
An announcement from Matex International Limited ( (SG:M15) ) is now available.

Matex International Limited, a company incorporated in Singapore, announced the discontinuance of two lawsuits, OA 733 and OA 790, following a court hearing and judgment. The court indicated that an injunction against the Subscription Agreements would have been granted if OA 733 had not been discontinued due to non-compliance with the company’s constitution. The trading suspension of the company’s securities remains in effect, and stakeholders are advised to stay informed through future announcements.

More about Matex International Limited

Average Trading Volume: 1,025,800

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: S$11.35M

data.title: Trinity One Metals Upsizes Private Placement to $750,000
data.description:
An update from Aranjin Resources ( (TSE:TOM) ) is now available.

Trinity One Metals Ltd., a company listed on the TSX Venture Exchange, has announced an increase in its non-brokered private placement from $600,000 to $750,000 due to strong investor demand. The funds raised will be used for exploring new growth opportunities, maintaining the company’s exploration portfolio, and general working capital. The offering includes issuing 15,000,000 units, each consisting of one common share and one warrant, with a related party transaction involving company insiders subscribing for 4,300,000 units.

More about Aranjin Resources

Average Trading Volume: 18,673

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: C$1.1M

data.title: Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Achieves Record NTA Growth
data.description:
Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd. ( (AU:FPC) ) has provided an announcement.

Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund has reported a significant increase in its estimated pre-tax NTA per share, reaching a record high above $1.84, marking an almost 9% rise in September. This growth is attributed to strong performances across all key investment themes, particularly in precious metals and Chinese technology stocks, indicating a bullish outlook for these sectors and attracting new shareholders.

More about Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd.

Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd. operates in the financial services industry, focusing on investment management. The company offers a contrarian investment fund that targets undervalued assets across various markets, with a significant emphasis on precious metals and Chinese equities.

data.title: Lynas Rare Earths Expands Market Presence with New Securities Quotation
data.description:
Lynas Rare Earths ( (AU:LYC) ) has shared an update.

Lynas Rare Earths Limited announced the application for quotation of 13,779,092 ordinary fully paid securities on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX). This move is part of a previously announced transaction, potentially enhancing the company’s financial flexibility and market presence. The issuance of these securities may impact Lynas’s operational capabilities and its strategic positioning within the rare earth industry, offering potential benefits to stakeholders.

Lynas Rare Earths Limited operates in the mining industry, focusing on the extraction and processing of rare earth elements. These elements are crucial for various high-tech applications, including electronics, renewable energy, and defense systems, positioning the company as a key player in the global rare earth market.

data.title: RPMGlobal Holdings Ltd Announces Cessation of Securities
data.description:
An update from RPMGlobal Holdings Ltd ( (AU:RUL) ) is now available.

RPMGlobal Holdings Ltd announced the cessation of 23,324 securities due to the expiry of options or other convertible securities without exercise or conversion. This development marks a routine adjustment in the company’s issued capital, reflecting the expiration of financial instruments that were not converted into shares, which may impact the company’s capital structure but is not expected to have significant implications for stakeholders.

data.title: Mandom Corporation Supports MBO Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
Mandom ( (JP:4917) ) has provided an update.

Mandom Corporation’s board has expressed support for a management buyout (MBO) by Kalon Holdings, recommending shareholders tender their shares despite market price fluctuations. The MBO aims to take the company private, with shares to be delisted, offering 1,960 yen per share, below the current market price.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:4917) stock is a Hold with a Yen2456.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mandom stock, see the JP:4917 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mandom

Mandom Corporation operates in the personal care industry, focusing on the production and distribution of cosmetics and grooming products. The company is listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange and is recognized for its innovative offerings in the beauty and personal care sector.

Average Trading Volume: 478,114

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen106.3B
